explanation.rule: The prefix out- is added to verbs to mean 'more than', 'better than', or 'beyond'. It often implies surpassing or exceeding someone or something in a particular quality or action.

groups.note: Means 'more than', 'better than', or 'beyond'; used to describe surpassing or exceeding others in some way

examples.definition: to do something better than someone else
to perform better than another person or thing
to be much better or more impressive than others
to be greater in number than another group
to gain an advantage over someone by being cleverer

wordForms.definition: to do something better than someone else
wordForms.example: Each year, the teams try to outdo each other with their creative costumes.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to carry out an action or task

wordForms.example: She received an award for her outstanding contribution to research.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to remain upright; to endure or tolerate

wordForms.definition: to perform or achieve better than someone or something else
wordForms.example: The new model consistently outperforms its competitors in safety tests.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to carry out a task, function, or action

wordForms.definition: to be much more impressive, skilled, or successful than others
wordForms.example: Her performance outshone all the other candidates at the audition.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to emit or reflect bright light

wordForms.definition: to gain an advantage over someone by being more clever or cunning
wordForms.example: The detective managed to outsmart the criminal at every turn.

wordForms.definition: to be greater in number than another group
wordForms.example: Female applicants outnumbered male applicants by three to one.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a quantity or value expressed numerically

wordForms.definition: to last or continue for longer than someone or something else
wordForms.example: This brand of battery is designed to outlast all its rivals.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to continue for a period of time; to endure

wordForms.definition: to grow too large or too mature for something; to grow faster than
wordForms.example: Children often outgrow their shoes within a few months.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to increase in size or develop over time

wordForms.definition: a description or summary of the main points or features of something
wordForms.example: Please prepare a brief outline of your proposal before the meeting.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a line that marks the boundary or shape of something

wordForms.definition: to live longer than another person or thing
wordForms.example: She outlived her husband by more than twenty years.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to remain alive or continue to exist

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: The young athlete managed to ___ all expectations and won a gold medal.
The new software consistently ___ older systems in speed and reliability.
In many cities, private cars ___ public transport users on the roads.
She always ___ her peers in class with her creative problem-solving skills.
Her ___ performance in the final exam earned her a scholarship.

transformExercises.sentence: Each competitor tried to ___ the others with a more impressive display. (DO)
The fox managed to ___ the hunters and escape into the forest. (SMART)
Volunteers ___ paid staff at the event by almost four to one. (NUMBER)
These ancient trees have ___ many generations of the families who planted them. (LAST)
Please ___ your main arguments before we begin the discussion. (LINE)
